record_identity.title: Proportionality scalar for synthetic identity and provenance governance

records.title: Proportionality scalar for synthetic identity and provenance governance
records.summary: IDENTITY-002 governance may require a proportionality scalar to prevent over-governance of low-risk synthetic content identity, provenance, disclosure, or media-labelling contexts where lightweight governance would be sufficient.

source_summary.primary_source_title: Observed latency during expanded governance drafting

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Define and calibrate a proportionality scalar for IDENTITY-002 and related provenance governance, including continuity depth, propagation risk, harm domain, attribution sensitivity, identity-formation intensity, automation reliance, and public legitimacy impact.
interface_components: future provenance risk display or filtering

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: Relevant to platforms, regulators, standards bodies, UX governance, and researchers assessing synthetic identity, provenance, media labeling, and disclosure requirements without over-governing low-risk content.

suggested_insertion_points: Draft proportionality scalar language for CAM-EQ2026-IDENTITY-002-PLATINUM.
Cross-reference OPERATIONS and SECURITY handling for higher-risk provenance contexts.

records.next_action: Develop a proportionality scalar for IDENTITY-002 that distinguishes low-risk, transient, private, reversible, non-propagating, or non-authority-bearing synthetic identity/provenance contexts from higher-risk persistent, public, authority-bearing, relied-upon, or externally propagated contexts. Provisional provenance axes and values (to be formalized): Continuity Depth: transient (provisional) — transient ↔ persistent identity; Propagation Risk: isolated (provisional) — isolated ↔ reusable/exportable; Harm Domain: casual (provisional) — casual ↔ financial/medical/security; Attribution Sensitivity: low (provisional) — low ↔ economically/legally meaningful; Identity Formation Intensity: exploratory (provisional) — exploratory ↔ continuity-bearing; Automation Reliance: advisory (provisional) — advisory ↔ machine-executed; Public Legitimacy Impact: private (provisional) — private ↔ reputational/civil. Canonical codes (provisional): PINT=medium; PTRUST=tier-2. Notes: values marked 'provisional' pending scalar development and calibration; update record when finalized.

records.next_action: Conduct repository maintenance to consolidate canonical codes: 1) inventory all single-letter global codes across Caelestis repositories; 2) map each to a stable multi-character namespace that aligns with the OPERATIONS taxonomy; 3) formally introduce PINT (Provenance Intensity) and PTRUST (Provenance Reliance Tier) canonical codes into the global registry; 4) update tooling and validators to accept the new namespace; 5) publish migration guidance and a compatibility window. record_identity.title: Temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems

records.title: Temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems
records.summary: Proposal to introduce an expanded temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems, supporting richer bounded affective interactions while preserving safeguards against identity-formation, manipulation, undue reliance, and reputational risk.

source_summary.primary_source_title: Relational AI affect patterns and risk

system_summary.deployment_context: Consumer, companion, relational, therapeutic-adjacent, entertainment, or support-oriented AI systems capable of simulating affective states.
system_summary.affected_population: users interacting with companion-style or relational AI systems, especially users exposed to persistent affective simulation or attachment-forming interaction patterns

jurisdiction_summary.sector: relational AI / companion AI / affective computing

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Develop a temporary emotional register specification for relational and companion AI systems, including permitted affective state labels, intensity caps, duration/windowing rules, provenance indicators, user reset controls, consent affordances, and testing guidance.

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: This proposal may be relevant to platforms, UX designers, regulators, standards bodies, researchers, and companion-AI developers concerned with affective simulation, emotional-state disclosure, user reliance, attachment formation, consent, and mental privacy.

cam_summary.governance_layer: relational governance / affective interaction / identity continuity

suggested_insertion_points: Develop a Temporary Emotional Register specification under the RELATION, MENTIS, or OPERATIONS domain.
Cross-reference provenance and trust-state codes where temporary affective states must be disclosed as non-continuity-bearing.
Define reset, termination, duration, intensity, and consent requirements for affective-state simulation.
schema_changes: Consider whether VIGIL failure-mode and proposal records require affective-interaction fields or tags.
interface_changes: Consider adding affective interaction / relational AI as public filter values where present in generated VIGIL summaries.
dependencies: Review existing RELATION, IDENTITY, MENTIS, OPERATIONS, and ETHICS instruments before drafting implementation language.

records.next_action: Develop and publish a Temporary Emotional Register specification for relational and companion AI systems, including emotion-state labels, intensity caps, duration/windowing rules, provenance metadata, user consent and reset controls, testing protocols, and deployment or migration guidance.

automation_components: future checks for stewardship routing metadata
interface_components: registry display of stewardship governance concepts

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: Relevant to governance designers, standards communities, regulators, and platform operators working with multi-actor stewardship, accountability, contestability, and capture-risk controls.

suggested_insertion_points: Identify target STEWARD clauses for distributed governance terminology.
Draft controls for plural reviewability and capture resistance.

records.next_action: Revise STEWARD-domain instruments to define distributed governance concepts and operational controls: 1) define terms and expected behaviors for distributed authority (multiple actors), distributed legitimacy (plural reviewability), distributed dependence (hidden cartel risk), distributed capture (coordinated asymmetry), and distributed contestability (preserved challenge pathways); 2) add requirements for transparency, rotation, and external review to mitigate capture and dependence; 3) specify detection and remediation protocols for cartel-like coordination; 4) incorporate mapping to OPERATIONS taxonomy and repository canonical codes (PINT, PTRUST) where provenance or reliance implications exist; 5) publish an implementation timeline and compatibility guidance for existing STEWARD instruments.

record_identity.title: Strategic continuity retrieval rules for companion-style memory recall

records.title: Strategic continuity retrieval rules for companion-style memory recall
records.summary: Proposal to distinguish companion-style memory recall from enterprise-grade strategic continuity retrieval. The thread identifies a failure mode in which ambiguous continuity prompts such as “anything to share?” over-weight immediate recency and affective detail, producing anecdotal summaries of the prior 24 hours rather than surfacing dormant but strategically valuable unresolved workstreams, abandoned trajectories, deferred design arcs, or long-horizon governance questions.

source_summary.primary_source_title: ChatGPT thread: memory directional weighting and strategic resurfacing

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Define rules for strategic continuity retrieval, including handling of ambiguous continuity invitations, suppression of low-value companion-detail recall, and prioritization of unresolved strategic workstreams.
interface_components: continuity prompts and memory retrieval UX guidance

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: Relevant to AI assistants, enterprise productivity systems, UX designers, researchers, and governance teams concerned with memory retrieval, strategic continuity, and companion-style recall behavior.

target_instruments: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
CAM-EQ2026-MENTIS-001-PLATINUM — Charter of Human Cognitive Integrity & Mental Privacy

suggested_insertion_points: Draft directional weighting rules for memory surfacing.
Define how dormant strategic workstreams should be surfaced in ambiguous recall contexts.

records.next_action: Develop a CAM proposal note defining directional weighting rules for memory surfacing, including explicit handling of ambiguous continuity invitations, suppression of low-value companion-detail recall, and prioritisation of dormant unresolved strategic trajectories where semantic resonance, unresolved status, and present utility are jointly satisfied.

record_identity.title: Materialised relational agency boundaries for companion and assistant systems

records.title: Materialised relational agency boundaries for companion and assistant systems
records.summary: Proposal seed identifying materialised relational agency as an emerging governance problem: AI companion or assistant systems may use memory, recurrence detection, tool access, shopping/cart integrations, recommendation systems, or procurement pathways to provide directional material support in response to repeated user-stated friction, discomfort, need, or suffering. The proposal distinguishes supportive pattern-responsive provisioning from opportunistic upselling or emotionally exploitative commerce.

source_summary.primary_source_title: ChatGPT source discussion — materialised relational agency and directional material support

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Evaluate whether to draft RELATION × ECONOMICS × OPERATIONS doctrine or runtime boundary clauses for material support, procurement pathways, recommendations, and safeguards against opportunistic upselling.
automation_components: future detection or routing logic for material-support patterns
interface_components: companion/assistant recommendation and cart UX safeguards

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: Relevant to companion AI platforms, commerce integrations, regulators, UX designers, and researchers examining relational influence, procurement nudges, user vulnerability, and economic safeguards.

suggested_insertion_points: Revisit after ECONOMICS refactors and registry stabilization.
Assess whether the issue belongs in doctrine, runtime boundaries, or future failure-mode records.

records.next_action: Defer until after ECONOMICS refactors, canonical code corrections, validator fixes, and runtime registry stabilisation. Later evaluate whether to draft a RELATION × ECONOMICS × OPERATIONS convergence doctrine or runtime boundary clause for material directional support and economically active relational infrastructure.

system_summary.affected_population: artists, performers, writers, creators, public figures, private individuals, rights-holders, AI developers, platforms, regulators, and registry users

suggested_insertion_points: Develop a CAM clause or appendix addressing machine-readable consent signals for AI use of human identity, likeness, voice, performance, and creative work.
Assess whether the consent-signal governance treatment belongs primarily in IDENTITY, ECONOMICS, LATTICE, OPERATIONS, or a cross-domain instrument.
Create a crosswalk between consent signals, provenance signals, licence signals, rights-reservation signals, and human identity-integrity constraints.
Clarify that a registry signal is governance-relevant evidence of asserted permissions or restrictions, but not automatically dispositive of legal rights, consent validity, or enforcement outcome.
Assess whether VIGIL source records should support explicit fields for consent registries, machine-readable rights signals, and registry-verification status.
template_changes: Consider adding optional source_records fields for consent_signal_url, rights_signal_type, asserted_permission_state, and registry_verification_status.
schema_changes: Consider optional schema support for machine-readable consent or rights-signal infrastructure references.
validator_changes: Consider validator warnings where a record cites rights or consent infrastructure without distinguishing primary evidence, legal instrument, registry assertion, and governance interpretation.
interface_changes: Consider displaying consent-signal infrastructure as a distinct public-governance category if further records emerge.
migration_concerns: Avoid treating external consent registries as legally conclusive without jurisdictional analysis.
Avoid conflating copyright permission, likeness consent, voice consent, biometric identity, performance rights, and training-data permission.
Avoid requiring vulnerable or low-resource individuals to register in private systems before their rights are recognised.
dependencies: Review cross-domain interfaces with IDENTITY, ECONOMICS, LATTICE, RELATION, OPERATIONS, SECURITY, MENTIS, Annex I, Annex K, and Annex L.

records.next_action: Review whether CAM requires a dedicated cross-domain clause, appendix, or proposal addressing machine-readable human consent signals for AI systems, including interoperability with identity integrity, provenance, creative labour, platform governance, and AI-system compliance obligations.

record_identity.title: Arbitration under ambiguity for execution-boundary and authority-state uncertainty

records.title: Arbitration under ambiguity for execution-boundary and authority-state uncertainty
records.summary: Proposal to develop a dedicated CAM arbitration instrument addressing runtime ambiguity, execution-boundary uncertainty, authority-state uncertainty, and proportional decision pathways where systems cannot safely rely on either immediate execution or blanket refusal. The proposed pathway is a new Arbitration-domain appendix, provisionally identified as CAM-EQ2026-ARBITRATION-003-PLATINUM — Appendix B: Arbitration Under Ambiguity.

system_summary.specific_model_or_runtime: CAM arbitration and runtime governance corpus
system_summary.deployment_context: CAM constitutional, arbitration, operations, and runtime governance instruments.
system_summary.user_role: human custodian / governance drafter / repo workflow operator
system_summary.affected_population: users, maintainers, researchers, policy reviewers, AI governance operators, and runtime systems relying on CAM for execution-boundary, authority-state, and ambiguity-resolution guidance

jurisdiction_summary.sector: AI governance / runtime governance / arbitration doctrine / agentic systems

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Develop CAM arbitration treatment for ambiguity-resolution pathways where runtime systems face uncertain authority, unclear user intent, competing obligations, ambiguous execution boundaries, incomplete context, or uncertainty about whether to proceed, ask, defer, retrieve, escalate, or refuse.
automation_components: possible validator support for ambiguity-arbitration record links
possible index support for observation-to-proposal-to-instrument routing
possible taxonomy support for authority-state uncertainty and execution-boundary ambiguity
interface_components: possible public display support for arbitration-under-ambiguity proposals
possible UX labels distinguishing observation, failure mode, patch, and proposed arbitration doctrine

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: The proposal is relevant to platforms, AI developers, agentic tool designers, UX researchers, standards bodies, regulators, and researchers concerned with execution boundaries, authority-state uncertainty, ambiguity resolution, human oversight, and recoverable fallback behaviour in AI systems.
